methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Beacon AI is a Series A-stage AI & Software company founded by Matt Cox, Avinash Nair, and Andrew Musto, having raised $15M+ to date. The peer universe consists of 9 comparables: 1 public (Palantir) and 8 private firms across the AI & Software sector, including Liquid AI, Field AI, and Twelve Labs.

Two valuation methods apply to this target. Stage-Progression uses the $15M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implying a $30M valuation. Peer-Median triangulates across three same-sector, same-stage comparables (Liquid AI at $2.4B, Field AI at $2.0B, and Twelve Labs at $250M), yielding a median peer valuation of $2.0B, with a range of $250M to $2.4B.

The two methods produce divergent signals. Blending Stage-Progression and Peer-Median results across their respective ranges yields a triangulated valuation spanning $26M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.0B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around whether Beacon AI's operational metrics and market traction align with high-valuation peers like Liquid AI or lower-valuation comparables such as Twelve Labs. Resolution depends on visibility into revenue, customer concentration, and product differentiation relative to the peer set.
